character: The main design goals with Crusader Kings III were: Character Focus : Crusader Kings is clearly and unequivocally about individual characters, unlike our other games.
A character belonged to a Dynasty, and that was that you got a minor opinion boost with characters that were of the same one, and nothing more.
Creating a Cadet Branch makes the character creating it House Head (with the most powerful House Head becoming Dynast), and by extension free from the direct influence of their old House Head.
faith: A House Head has the power to legitimize bastards, call House members to war, and demand that they adhere to their Faith (refusal to convert will result in them creating a new Cadet Branch).
This week, we're going to zoom in on one particular slice of the new expansion features, where your character starts having an actual relationship with their faith.
We will look over our revisions to Tenets , Spiritual Fulfillment , Personal Tenets , and the new Study Faith scheme.
court: In the meantime, please enjoy Royal Court and will have more info for you in the weeks to come!
Since this system works a bit like, e.g., Court Grandeur, you can gain effects that temporarily increase it beyond the baseline.
A Tenet you practice openly what isn't Prohibited in your Faith becomes available to close family automatically, and may also spread to members of your court and other relations you have.
realm: Marrying in such a way that your Dynasty ends up on the throne of a foreign realm is therefore useful for other reasons than to just murder them until you inherit their lands.
For those of you who are unacquainted with me and my work, I ply my trade in the mysterious and often dangerous realm of User Experience.
Zoom in even further and you’ll see the names of all the counties along with the terrain, as we strip away the realm colors.
culture: You will also be able to become a Merchant via a Decision when playing as a Duke or Count, as long as your Culture allows it.

The Faith and Culture lists were transformed into a big list of dropdown menus, where players can expand and collapse the dropdowns as they wish and highlight each one of the cultures/ faiths and see their tooltip for information on it.
These will vary greatly across the world, but are generally specialised in the conditions of warfare that’s typical for the culture in question.
war: A House Head has the power to legitimize bastards, call House members to war, and demand that they adhere to their Faith (refusal to convert will result in them creating a new Cadet Branch).
You can be a creditor, a war profiteer, or perhaps a caravaneer who has an easier time engaging in longer trade routes.
The amount of provinces will be noticeable when waging war, as it offers a larger degree of movement for you armies (more on that in the future).
